0:["6POe0U4NLJKUK3ANLy6UQ",[[["",{"children":["tools",{"children":["heart-rate-calculator",{"children":["__PAGE__",{}]}]}]},"$undefined","$undefined",true],["",{"children":["tools",{"children":["heart-rate-calculator",{"children":["__PAGE__",{},[["$L1",[["$","main",null,{"className":"min-h-screen bg-background","children":["$","section",null,{"className":"pt-24 pb-12 md:pt-32 md:pb-16","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"max-w-2xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"text-center mb-12","children":[["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-4xl md:text-5xl font-bold text-foreground mb-4","children":"Heart Rate Calculator"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-lg text-muted-foreground max-w-2xl mx-auto","children":"Calculate your target heart rate zones for fat burn, cardio, and peak performance."}]]}],["$","$L2",null,{}]]}]}]}],["$","$L3",null,{"title":"Understanding Heart Rate Zones: Complete Guide","children":["$","article",null,{"className":"prose prose-gray max-w-none","children":[["$","h2",null,{"children":"What Are Heart Rate Zones?"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Heart rate zones are ranges of beats per minute (BPM) that correspond to different training intensities. Training in specific zones helps you achieve different fitness goals more efficiently."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"The Five Heart Rate Zones"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Zone 1 (Very Light)"}],": 50-60% of max HR — Recovery and warm-up"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Zone 2 (Light)"}],": 60-70% of max HR — Fat burning and endurance base"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Zone 3 (Moderate)"}],": 70-80% of max HR — Aerobic fitness and efficiency"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Zone 4 (Hard)"}],": 80-90% of max HR — Anaerobic capacity and threshold"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Zone 5 (Maximum)"}],": 90-95% of max HR — Peak performance and speed"]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"How to Calculate Max Heart Rate"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["The most common formula is: ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"Max HR = 220 - age"}],". While simple, this is an estimate. For more accuracy, consider a stress test or use the Tanaka formula: 208 - (0.7 × age)."]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Why Zone Training Matters"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Fat Burn (Zone 2)"}],": Your body uses a higher percentage of fat for fuel at lower intensities"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Cardio Health (Zone 3)"}],": Builds aerobic base and improves heart efficiency"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Performance (Zones 4-5)"}],": Increases lactate threshold and VO2 max"]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Tips for Effective Heart Rate Training"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"Invest in a reliable heart rate monitor (chest strap or optical wrist sensor)"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Spend 80% of training time in Zones 1-2 for endurance building"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Use Zones 4-5 sparingly (about 20% of training) to avoid overtraining"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Factor in heart rate drift — your HR rises over long sessions even at constant pace"}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Related Tools"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","a",null,{"href":"/tools/pace-calculator","children":"Pace Calculator"}]," — Calculate your running pace and split times"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","a",null,{"href":"/tools/bmr-calculator","children":"BMR Calculator"}]," — Find your daily calorie burn at rest"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","a",null,{"href":"/tools/calorie-calculator","children":"Calorie Calculator"}]," — Estimate total daily energy expenditure"]}]]}]]}]}]],null],null],null]},[null,["$","$L4",null,{"parallelRouterKey":"children","segmentPath":["children","tools","children","heart-rate-calculator","children"],"error":"$undefined","errorStyles":"$undefined","errorScripts":"$undefined","template":["$","$L5",null,{}],"templateStyles":"$undefined","templateScripts":"$undefined","notFound":"$undefined","notFoundStyles":"$undefined"}]],null]},[null,["$","$L4",null,{"parallelRouterKey":"children","segmentPath":["children","tools","children"],"error":"$undefined","errorStyles":"$undefined","errorScripts":"$undefined","template":["$","$L5",null,{}],"templateStyles":"$undefined","templateScripts":"$undefined","notFound":"$undefined","notFoundStyles":"$undefined"}]],null]},[[[["$","link","0",{"rel":"stylesheet","href":"/_next/static/css/08337a2139cd8e6c.css","precedence":"next","crossOrigin":"$undefined"}],["$","link","1",{"rel":"stylesheet","href":"/_next/static/css/be3aed8f55a64b30.css","precedence":"next","crossOrigin":"$undefined"}]],["$","html",null,{"lang":"en","className":"__variable_f367f3
